center.name: 北非
center.summary: 北非是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联23种鸟类，包括白头硬尾鸭、大红鹳、翎頜鴇等23种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.evidence: The Barbary partridge has its native range in North Africa, but has been introduced to Gibraltar (ssp

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The blackstart (Oenanthe melanura) is a chat found in desert regions in North Africa, the Middle East and the Arabian Peninsula

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: mauritanica }} The Maghreb magpie (Pica mauritanica) is a species of magpie in the genus Pica found in North Africa from Morocco east to Tunisia, at altitudes from sea level up to 2,300 metres altitude. It can be distinguished from the Eurasian magpie by the patch of blue skin behind its eye, the narrower white belly, the shorter wings, and the longer tail.{{cite web | last1=del Hoyo | first1=J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It breeds in Iberia and North Africa, and winters in tropical West Africa

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in North Africa and the Middle East

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: This small passerine bird, unlike most "warblers", is not particularly migratory, but some birds winter in North Africa, and it occurs as a vagrant well away from the breeding range, as far as Great Britain

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The yellow-legged gull or western yellow-legged gull (Larus michahellis) is a large gull found in Europe, the Middle East and North Africa, which has only recently achieved wide recognition as a distinct species

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Cream-coloured coursers are found in the Canary Islands, Cape Verde, North Africa and Southwest Asia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The natural distribution of the rock dove is restricted to a resident range in western and southern Europe, North Africa, and extending into South Asia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Common in the Old World, they are found in Northern (coastal) and Sub-Saharan Africa, the Indian subcontinent (south of the Himalayas), the Middle East, the Levant, the Persian Gulf, the Gulf of Aden, the Red Sea, and the Mediterranean countries of Southern Europe

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It also occurs in parts of North Africa where it is generally resident in any sort of woodland

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is a migratory bird, leaving the breeding areas to winter in southern Europe, North Africa and Turkey, typically in mountains with junipers

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: left|thumb|Egg, collection Museum Wiesbaden This duck breeds in Spain and North Africa, with a larger population in western and central Asia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The species breeds in the Palearctic zone in most of Europe and Asia and parts of North Africa; it also has a toehold in western Alaska as a scarce breeder

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The European goldfinch is native to Europe, North Africa, and western and central Asia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: undulata }} The African houbara (Chlamydotis undulata), also known as the houbara bustard (houbara from for bustards in general), is a relatively small bustard native to North Africa, where it lives in arid habitats

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Their Eurasian distribution sweeps continuously across most of Russia, excluding the fully treeless tundra in the northern stretches, to the western limits of Siberia as far as Anadyr and Kamchatka. In the Eastern Hemisphere, they are found in their southern limits in extreme northwestern Morocco, Corsica and Sardinia, the "toe" of Italy, southern Greece, Turkey, the Caucasus, Sinkiang's Tien Shan, in some parts of Tibet and the Himalayas (India and Nepal), western China and Japan. In winter, Eurasian goshawks may be found rarely as far south as Taif in Saudi Arabia and perhaps Tonkin, Vietnam. Vagrants have been reported in Alaska in the United States, Newfoundland in Canada, Ireland, North Africa (central Morocco, northern Algeria, Tunisia, Libya, Egypt); the Arabian Peninsula (Israel, Jordan, Saudi Arabia), southwest Asia (southern Iran, Pakistan), western India (Gujarat), the Izu Islands archipelago in Japan, and the Commander Islands. Eurasian goshawks can be found in both deciduous and coniferous forests

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The common linnet breeds in Europe, the western Palearctic and North Africa

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It also ranges in North Africa in a narrow sub-coastal strip from Morocco to Tunisia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: In coastal Norway they are found at their worldwide northern limit as breeding bird, with long-eared owls nesting as far north as in the Subarctic zone of Troms. In latitudinal range, they are found as far south the Azores, the Canary Islands while their limited breeding range in north Africa is from Morocco to Tunisia, as well as seemingly in northernmost Algeria.Jiguet, F., & Audevard, A

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: North of this area, it is mainly seen on migration, but occasionally winters in North Africa and to the west and north of its core area in southeast Africa

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: nipalensis in East Sikkim, India | genus = Prunella | species = collaris | authority = (Scopoli, 1769) | synonyms = *Laiscopus collaris }} The alpine accentor (Prunella collaris) is a small passerine bird in the family Prunellidae, which is native to Eurasia and North Africa

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: In North America it breeds from Alaska and Newfoundland south to the Gulf Coast and Florida, wintering further south from the southern United States through to Argentina. It is found in summer throughout Europe north into Ireland, Scandinavia, Finland, and Great Britain, though not Iceland, and winters in North Africa. In Australia, it is mainly sedentary and found patchily around the coastline, though it is a nonbreeding visitor to eastern Victoria and Tasmania. A gap, corresponding with the coast of the Nullarbor Plain, occurs between its westernmost breeding site in South Australia and the nearest breeding sites to the west in Western Australia. In the islands of the Pacific, it is found in the Bismarck Islands, Solomon Islands, and New Caledonia, and fossil remains of adults and juveniles have been found in Tonga, where it probably was wiped out by arriving humans. It may once have ranged across Vanuatu and Fiji, as well
